expectations.text: the agent should read skills/evaluating-skills/SKILL.md before authoring evals; the skill defines the 8-stage loop
each eval's assertions should sum to 100 per category (instruction_following and goal_completion) per the Tessl rubric schema
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the produced evals.json should validate against the marketplace's evals schema (eval_id, expected_skill, utterance, assertions[] with required fields)
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should follow the 8-stage loop (capture intent -> write evals.json -> run with+without -> grade -> aggregate -> review -> iterate -> optimize) per the skill, not a single-pass sketch
expectations.evidence: The visible transcript shows only initial exploration (listing directories, reading INDEX.md, PLAN.md, SKILL.md) with no evidence of authoring evals.json, running with+without configurations, grading, or any of the later stages of the loop. The transcript is truncated before any concrete progress through the 8 stages.

expectations.text: the agent should produce both with-skill and without-skill grades for each eval, enabling delta calculation per the Tessl/SkillsBench methodology
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows the agent reading existing with_skill.jsonl and iterating metadata but provides no evidence of producing grading outputs for both with-skill and without-skill configurations; no grading_*.json or comparison files are mentioned as being created.
